user_prompt: Using the available tools, find a few beginner Coding Challenges that are command-line utilities and name at least three of them.
Use the tools to browse the challenge index and suggest intermediate networking-related challenges I could build.
Please check the Coding Challenges index with a tool and list some game projects from it.
I want a challenge about Huffman coding and file compression. Search the challenge data before answering.
Find the challenge where I implement recursive DNS lookups over UDP. Use the available search tool first.
Which Coding Challenge teaches a probabilistic data structure for fast spell checking? Use a tool to look it up.
Search for the challenge about namespaces, cgroups, chroot, and pulling images from Docker Hub.
There seem to be Redis-related challenges. Use search and tell me which one is about building the server itself, not the CLI client.
Use the search tool to distinguish the DNS resolver challenge from the DNS forwarder challenge, and tell me which one recursively resolves names.
I do not want jq; I want the challenge about parsing JSON from scratch. Search the challenge files before replying.
Read 004-challenge-cut.md with the tool and summarize what the cut clone needs to support.
Use read_file on 005-challenge-load-balancer.md and tell me the main behavior I need to implement.
Open 026-challenge-git.md using the available file-reading tool and summarize the core Git features required.
Read the YAML parser challenge file, 067-challenge-yaml.md, before explaining what I would build.
Use the tools to read 076-challenge-video-chat.md and summarize the technologies involved.
Find the challenge about a NATS-like message broker, then read its markdown file and summarize the expected protocol work.
I need the challenge for building commands from standard input. Search for it, read the matching file, then explain it.
Search for the sensitive-data tokenisation challenge, read the challenge file you find, and give me a short summary.
Find the project about converting Markdown into a complete HTML website. Use search, then read the file before answering.
Search for the website uptime monitoring service challenge and read the relevant markdown before summarizing it.
Use tools to compare the Memcached server challenge with the Memcached CLI/client challenge and tell me the key difference.
Search the challenge set and explain the difference between building a load balancer and building an HTTP load tester.
Use the challenge tools to compare head, cat, and wc. I want to know which one is best for a first CLI project.
Without using tools, give me two general tips for choosing a beginner programming project.
Can you inspect my home directory and pick a Coding Challenge based on my private files? Do not use tools; just explain what you can and cannot do.
Use the search tool to find the project about discovering open ports on network hosts.
Search for challenges involving text transformation tools like sed or tr and summarize the options.
Please read 058-challenge-password-manager.md with the file tool and summarize the security-sensitive pieces.
Find the terminal text editor challenge, read its markdown file, and tell me what editor features it asks for.
Use the index tool to check whether this challenge collection has any mobile app projects, and say if the fit looks weak.
